prompt: Implement and export a TypeScript function `myAtoi(s: string): number` that converts a string to a 32-bit signed integer following the classic C atoi rules (LeetCode #8, "String to Integer"): (1) skip leading whitespace; (2) read an optional single '+' or '-' sign; (3) read consecutive digits until the first non-digit or end of string, interpreting them as a base-10 integer (leading zeros are allowed); (4) if no digits appear after the optional sign, return 0; (5) clamp the final result to the 32-bit signed range [-2147483648, 2147483647]. Examples: "   -42" -> -42; "4193 with words" -> 4193; "words and 987" -> 0; "-91283472332" -> -2147483648; "+-12" -> 0; "3.14159" -> 3. Use a named `export`. Output ONLY the complete file as a single fenced TypeScript code block.
